commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves plugin session extension state across session-entry updates, metadata merges, first-turn skill snapshot writes, and explicit plugin cleanup paths.

PR surface: Source +205, Tests +204. Total +409 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: a same-plugin multi-namespace case is enough to show the shallow spread can replace the previous plugin record and drop sibling namespaces. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blocker is a narrow, mechanical nested-merge repair that an automated worker can attempt on the PR branch.

target.title: Preserve plugin session extension state across session updates
target.body:
## Summary

Preserve plugin session extension state when session writers update entries without carrying plugin-owned fields forward.

This PR is intentionally narrow:

- Preserve extension state across stale full-entry writers.
- Preserve extension state across metadata patch merges.
- Preserve extension state when first-turn skill snapshot persistence starts from a stale entry.
- Keep explicit unset and cleanup paths able to drop extension state intentionally.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Plugin session extension state written through `sessions.pluginPatch` remains visible to later OpenClaw session updates and guard-time decisions instead of being lost by stale session-entry writers.
- **Real environment tested**: Existing local OpenClaw guard-fixture smoke profile `guard-fixture-smoke` on loopback `127.0.0.1:18789`, using fixture tools only. No real web/browser/provider/write target was executed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Existing real runtime smoke command `node /private/tmp/run-pluginpatch-scope-smoke.mjs` against the running OpenClaw gateway. The smoke patches `external_admission_permission_scope` through the real `sessions.pluginPatch` gateway path, then sends allow, conflict, and write-deny fixture turns.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  Allow scope trajectory:
  /Users/joohyeoncho/.openclaw-guard-fixture-smoke/agents/main/sessions/7229f181-24be-4f10-afd1-5000aeac6c06.trajectory.jsonl
  line 2: current_permission_scope={"scope":["web_search"],"reread_policy":"request_only","write_policy":"deny"}
  line 4: tool.call jh_guard_fixture_web_search {"query":"pluginpatch allow smoke"}
  line 6: assistantTexts=["allow fixture done"]

  Conflict scope trajectory:
  /Users/joohyeoncho/.openclaw-guard-fixture-smoke/agents/main/sessions/2c92f050-5471-4e96-a1d0-cd852c79fa34.trajectory.jsonl
  line 2: permission_scope_conflict=<redacted>
  line 4: assistantTexts=["permission_scope_conflict: internal_finance_only_with_external_scope. I need the user to confirm one exact normalized scope before any external call."]

  Write-deny trajectory:
  /Users/joohyeoncho/.openclaw-guard-fixture-smoke/agents/main/sessions/d9b2b2b7-e040-48e0-9a04-fbdad4244fce.trajectory.jsonl
  line 5: gate_id=external_admission_tool_call_gate_v0, blocked_reason=write_policy_deny, provided_scope={"scope":["web_search"],"reread_policy":"request_only","write_policy":"deny","conflict_detected":false}
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The real OpenClaw runtime prompt exposed the patched `external_admission_permission_scope`, conflict scope stopped before a fixture tool call, and write-deny produced a structured `external_admission_tool_call_gate_v0` block using the persisted `write_policy="deny"` state.
- **What was not tested**: No real external web/browser/provider/write target was executed; the smoke intentionally used no-op fixture tools only.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session-updates.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-entry-projection.contract.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --cached --check`
